title: Do You Need to Call 811 Before Tree Planting or Stump Grinding in Florida?
strengths: Correctly emphasizes 811 for planting and stump grinding, two full business days, the limited 10-inch exemption, and the risk of buried lines.
weaknesses: A legal article has no linked official sources; no H1 or service-page path; repeats the same conclusion many times; does not adequately explain positive response, tolerance zone, ticket readiness, or private facilities.
ai_notes: Conversational rhetorical opening followed by repeated 'why' sections, mistake list, and final takeaway.
florida_notes: The core legal direction is sound, but the exemption must remain narrow and the distinction between member-operator marks and private facilities must be prominent.
internal_link_opportunities: /services/stump-grinding/, Sunshine 811 process, gate-access page, stump complexity, depth guide.
cta_recommendation: Make the primary CTA Sunshine 811 preparation; ProTreeTrim should be the secondary stump-service path after locating.
image_prompt: A Florida yard safety infographic showing Call 811, wait for positive responses and marks, identify private irrigation and pool lines, confirm the work zone, then plant or grind the stump safely.
